title: Event-Driven Programming Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the EventEmitter class (.on/.emit) and the architectural benefit of decoupling code for maintainability in Node.js.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy behind 'Event-Driven' programming in Node.js?
options: A system focused on listening for and reacting to specific 'events' (like a database finishing or a user logging in).
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily heart of the Node module system for handling events?
Which method in the 'EventEmitter' class is used to 'signal' or trigger an event that should be handled by other parts of the app?
Which and/or what method is used in the 'EventEmitter' class to register a 'listener' that should run whenever a specific event occurs?
Does following the 'Event-Driven' model help 'decouple' your code and make it easier to maintain in a professional development environment?
options: Yes, as it allows for many small, independent listeners to react to the same architectural events.
